description: Implement Paradigm Forking / Self-Redesign - Crisis Detection and Paradigm Fork Protocol with safety guards, integrated with identity runtime
steps: Create virtual_genesis/runtime/identity_runtime/crisis_detector.py with detect_crisis(anomaly_history: List[Dict], theory_failures: int, drift_score: float) -> dict function. Returns CrisisReport dict with: level (str: 'normal'/'warning'/'crisis'), anomaly_count (int), theory_failure_count (int), drift_score (float), reasons (List[str]). Crisis conditions: warning when anomaly_count >= 3 OR drift_score > 0.5; crisis when anomaly_count >= 5 AND theory_failures >= 2 AND drift_score > 0.6.

Create virtual_genesis/runtime/identity_runtime/paradigm_fork.py with two functions: propose_fork(crisis_report: dict, identity: AgentIdentityObject) -> dict returns ForkProposal dict with: proposed_changes (list of commitment changes), preserved (list - lineage + successful concepts), discarded (list - failed theories, low-utility memories), justification (str), fork_id (str via make_id). execute_fork(proposal: dict, identity: AgentIdentityObject) -> dict returns ForkResult dict with: success (bool), new_identity (AgentIdentityObject with updated commitments + lineage appended), archived_policies (list), reset_drift_score (float=0.0).

Add fork safety in paradigm_fork.py: (1) execute_fork refuses without justification in proposal (returns success=False), (2) A module-level MINIMUM_CYCLES_BETWEEN_FORKS=10 constant - execute_fork checks identity.meta['last_fork_cycle'] and refuses if fewer than 10 cycles passed, (3) preserve all items in identity.lineage, (4) ForkProposal.discarded must not overlap with preserved.

Wire crisis detection and paradigm fork into pipeline: Add use_paradigm_fork: bool = False parameter to run_minimal_pipeline. When enabled, after anomaly_candidates extraction: build anomaly_history from store, count theory_failures from theory_registry, call detect_crisis. If crisis level is 'crisis' and identity is provided, call propose_fork then execute_fork, update identity object. Store crisis_report and fork_result in pipeline return dict.
Ensure all existing 234 tests still pass with the new code (default off, no behavior change).
acceptance_criteria: detect_crisis returns 'normal' for low anomaly counts and low drift
detect_crisis returns 'warning' when anomaly_count >= 3 or drift > 0.5
detect_crisis returns 'crisis' when all three conditions met (anomaly >= 5, failures >= 2, drift > 0.6)
propose_fork generates a valid proposal with preserved and discarded items
execute_fork creates new identity with updated commitments and appended lineage
Fork safety: refuses without justification, respects minimum cycle gap, preserves lineage
Pipeline includes crisis_report and fork_result when enabled and crisis detected
verification: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q (all 234 existing + new paradigm fork tests pass)
findings: All 285 tests pass (264 existing + 21 new paradigm fork tests). Crisis detector, paradigm fork with safety guards, and pipeline integration all working. Gated behind use_paradigm_fork=False default. No breaking changes.
